column2: Guideline
Swagger naming convention should be as per the given micro-service
Group the similar APIs logically and put in separate sections in the same Swagger file
Server URLs for all the envs are given
column3:
servers:
  - url: 'https://apidev/b2b' 
    description: DEV
  - url: 'https://apisit/b2b'
    description: SIT
  - url: 'https://apipreprod/b2b'
    description: PREPROD
  - url: 'https://api/b2b'
    description: PROD

column2: For each API
Summary of the API should be simple and short
column3: E.g., Check if user name exists

column2: OperationId is in camelcase and is short in length (not too long)
API URLs must be lower case. Use hyphen for better readability if there are multi-word api names (e.g., /user-mgmt)
URL parameters in the URL (in: path) are provided correctly, though it is preferred to have request body instead of URL parameters for sensitive fields
Query parameters in the URL (in: query) are provided correctly, though it is preferred to have request body instead of query parameters for sensitive fields
APIs having get functionality but with request payload will use POST method. Such API URLs to contain /get in the end. This is due to sensitive fields cannot be part of the URL
It is recommended not to place the API version at the end of the URL. If needed use /manage in the end
Sensitive fields should not be used in URLs / query
column3: E.g., mobile no., account no etc.

column2: Request attributes having enum should have the list of possible values for validation
Use string type for integer values. E.g., MSISDN (Mobile number), Code and Account numbers etc. should be used as string, instead of numeric types
Use HTTP POST instead of DELETE due to security reasons

Description should be multi-line (clean) and needs to include
- Summary
- DB Table

If "DB Table" section not applicable, remove the same
column3:
description: |
        **Summary:**

        Caller will request to resend the OTP using this API. This API will re-generate the OTP and calls SMS GW API to deliver it to the user.

        **DB Tables:**
        - OTP_DETAILS

column2: Response payload should be divided into "status" & "data" objects. "data" should be an object instead of direct string reference as per the example given
Reuse of the request & response schema objects across multiple API definitions in the same swagger file
All APIs to have both request & response header blocks (parameters: & headers:) as below
column3:
Correlation-Id:
      name: Correlation-Id
      in: header
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
        example: 89318246-5802-42cc-8f3d-9232348cf64e
      description: Correlation ID of the (Correlation ID for the ongoing journey, in case of direct/single API call without journey, correlation and transaction ID can share the same value)
    Source:
      name: Source
      in: header
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
        example: "B2BPortal, B2BIOS, B2BAndroid"
      description: Mandatory attribute to identify the caller.
    Language:
      name: Language
      in: header
      required: false
      schema:
        type: string
        enum: ['en', 'es']
        example: en
      description: Language selected by the user

column2: Schema related guidelines are listed below:
Use underscores/hyphen for enum / list types. Spaces should not be used
Request & response payload attributes should be camelCase
Each swagger to have below schemas for Status object which will be part of response object
column3:
Status:
      type: object
      description: Model Represents Status
      properties:
        code:
          type: string
          description: Possible status code / error codes
          enum:
            - MAPI-400
            - MAPI-000
          example: API-400
        message:
          type: string
          description: title of error message
          example: Invalid input

column2: All requests and responses must have "sample" examples and should be visible in Swagger Editor
Title of the request / response examples in Swagger Editor should be meaningful. E.g., instead of keeping as example, example2 keep it as given
Business validation errors should be categorized as (460). Make sure that 460-Error-Response has correct examples. Multiple business validation errors should be names as 460, 460_1, 460_2, 460_3
Ensure all the responses have the header (Correlation-Id)
For complex scenarios, the payload may be made extensible to handle futuristic usecases
No need to use the error codes which normally is the responsibility of API GW (E.g., 405, 407, 413, 414, 429, 431, 502, 503, 504)
